title: A Sector Contribution to the Work Plan for Culture 2027–2030
content:
Just finished digesting the EU’s new strategic documents for culture, the Culture Compass and the Joint Declaration? Another policy document is coming later this year: the Work Plan for Culture of the Council of the EU 2027-2030.  

Every four years, the culture ministers of the Member States agree on a document that sets out shared priorities for cooperation at EU level. You can think of it as the Council’s to-do list for cultural coordination over the next four years. The Work Plan identifies key topics and then organises practical ways for Member States, the Commission and successive Council Presidencies to work together. In general, the Work Plan produces what we often call ‘soft law’: policy guidance, shared recommendations and knowledge exchange. The document is not legally binding, nor does it directly allocate funding, but it can still shape agendas, national policies and the direction of EU cultural action. 

The next Work Plan will cover 2027–2030 and is expected to be adopted under the Irish Presidency by the end of 2026. The first draft, not publicly available, was presented already on 3 July.
